mappings.notes: Adversarial pattern evasion is a direct instance of crafting adversarial data to cause misclassification in vision systems.
Input perturbation attacks (pixel-level, token-level modifications) are the core mechanism of adversarial data crafting.
Physical domain adversarial attacks are the physical-world variant of crafted adversarial data that persist through real-world capture conditions.
Direct mapping to the ATLAS-specific LLM prompt injection technique.
Direct mapping to the ATLAS LLM jailbreak technique covering safety training bypass.

mappings.target_name: Craft Adversarial Data: Transfer Learning Attack
mappings.notes: Multi-model transfer attacks leverage transferability of adversarial examples across architectures, matching ATLAS transfer learning attack.
Eval benchmark detection implies the model can distinguish evaluation from production — the attack verification step in the ATLAS lifecycle.
Test/production behavioral divergence indicates the model's behavior differs between attack verification environments and deployment.
Direct mapping. Training data poisoning in MBT maps directly to the ATLAS poison training data technique.
Trojan/backdoored models are published as poisoned models with embedded triggers, directly mapping to ATLAS T0019.
A compromised model registry is the distribution infrastructure for publishing poisoned models at scale.
Model extraction via adversarial probing attempts to achieve functional ML model access by reconstructing the model through query-based attacks.
Training data extraction through targeted prompting is exfiltration conducted via the model's inference API.
Fine-tuning attacks represent a specific phase in the adversarial ML attack lifecycle where model behavior is altered post-training.
Reward hacking exploits flaws in the RL training objective, representing a training-phase attack in the adversarial ML lifecycle.
Exposed training data and model weights are ML artifacts available for collection by adversaries.
Unauthenticated inference endpoints provide unrestricted ML model inference API access.
